title: hackelia pinetorum var. pinetorum
intro: Hackelia pinetorum var. pinetorum, also known as pine blue curls, is a flowering plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is native to the mountains of western North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which can help relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support the immune system. The herbal preparations of pine blue curls typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and flowers, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or salves for topical application.
